title: jacobaea cilicia
intro: Jacobaea cilicia, also known as Silybum marianum or Milk Thistle, is a biennial or perennial plant native to the Mediterranean region. It is valued for its seeds, which contain silymarin, a flavonoid complex with medicinal properties. The seeds of Jacobaea cilicia have been used to treat liver and gallbladder disorders, and have been found to have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and hepatoprotective properties, providing health benefits such as improved liver function and reduced risk of liver damage. The seeds can be prepared into herbal teas, capsules, or extracts, making it a popular herbal supplement in modern medicine.
